qualifications.degree_school_row_2: Associate of Applied Science in Health Information Management
qualifications.degree_other_row: Certificate in Medical Office Administration and Practice Management
qualifications.address_city_state_other_row: Online / Continuing Education program based in Salem, OR

special_skills.special_skills_or_experience:
Technical & Software Skills:
- Proficient in electronic health record (EHR) systems, including Epic (3 years), Athenahealth (1 year), and eClinicalWorks (basic familiarity). I regularly used Epic at my current clinic to schedule appointments, verify insurance, document patient check-in and check-out, and manage provider calendars.
- Advanced user of Microsoft Office Suite: Word (templates, mail merge for patient letters, formatting policies and procedures), Excel (creating and maintaining spreadsheets for tracking no-show rates, insurance authorizations, and monthly visit volumes; comfortable with VLOOKUP, pivot tables, and basic charts), Outlook (calendar coordination across providers, creating shared calendars and meeting invites), and PowerPoint (developed short training decks for new front-desk staff).
- Comfortable with Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Drive, and shared team folders), Adobe Acrobat (filling and editing PDFs, combining scanned documents), and basic database usage through clinic billing software.
- Typing speed consistently around 70–75 WPM with high accuracy; accustomed to entering detailed patient information while maintaining eye contact and rapport.

Healthcare & Administrative Experience:
- Over 5 years of experience in medical office and clinic front-desk roles, including patient registration, insurance verification, referral coordination, and managing multi-line phone systems.
- Experienced in handling confidential patient information in compliance with HIPAA; I completed annual HIPAA training at my last two employers and was often the person coworkers asked when they had questions about what could or could not be shared.
- Familiar with common commercial and government insurance plans (Regence BlueCross, Providence, Kaiser, Moda, Medicare, Medicaid/OHP). I routinely verified eligibility, collected co-pays, and explained basic coverage questions to patients in an accessible way without giving formal billing advice.
- Comfortable working in a fast-paced clinic with high patient volume (average 60–80 patients per day across multiple providers) while maintaining calm, professional communication and attention to detail.
- Experience coordinating referrals to specialty clinics, imaging centers, and physical therapy offices, including obtaining prior authorizations, faxing clinical notes, and following up on approvals or denials.

Communication & Customer Service:
- Bilingual in English and Spanish (native Spanish speaker, fluent in English). I frequently interpret basic conversations for Spanish-speaking patients (scheduling, instructions, paperwork) and help translate written reminders and instructions under supervision.
- Recognized informally by providers and coworkers as someone who can de-escalate tense situations at the front desk. For example, I developed a personal approach of acknowledging frustration, clarifying what we can do today, and offering concrete next steps, which significantly reduced the number of complaints escalating to management.
- Comfortable explaining clinic policies (late arrivals, missed appointments, payment plans) in a way that is firm but empathetic, especially for patients who are anxious or confused.
- Strong written communication skills; I have drafted patient letters (appointment reminders, referral information, and general notices) and edited internal procedures for clarity.

Leadership, Training, and Process Improvement:
- Served as an informal lead for a team of three front-desk staff at my current clinic. I helped create weekly task lists, ensured coverage for lunch breaks, and was often responsible for training new hires on check-in/check-out workflows and EHR basics.
- Led a small process improvement project to reduce check-in times by organizing commonly used forms into clearly labeled, color-coded folders and creating a short checklist for new patient registrations. After these changes, average check-in time for new patients dropped from about 10–12 minutes to around 7–8 minutes, based on our informal tracking.
- Helped implement a reminder-call and text-message workflow: I coordinated with our billing specialist and IT contact to adjust the schedule report, then trained staff to make reminder calls two days before appointments, which noticeably reduced no-show rates.
- Regularly volunteered to stay a bit late at the end of the day to ensure all charts were closed, lab results routed correctly, and the front desk area was organized and ready for the next morning.

Organizations, Volunteering, and Professional Development:
- Volunteer Patient Liaison, Salud para Todos Free Clinic, Portland, OR (2022–present, 1–2 evenings per month). I assist with Spanish-speaking patient intake, help patients complete forms, and coordinate with volunteer providers.
- Member, Oregon Medical Office Professionals Network (informal local group). I attended quarterly meetups (virtual during the pandemic and in-person more recently) and short workshops on topics like conflict resolution at the front desk, compassionate communication, and understanding prior authorizations.
- Completed multiple continuing education webinars on topics such as: "Fundamentals of Revenue Cycle in Outpatient Clinics," "Best Practices for Front-Desk Collections," and "Managing Burnout in Healthcare Support Roles."

Personal Strengths Relevant to the Role:
- Highly organized and detail-oriented; I keep a small notebook where I track daily to-do items, follow-ups on referrals, and questions to ask providers or managers so that nothing falls through the cracks.
- Comfortable learning new software and systems; at my last job, I was one of the first staff members to fully adopt a new online check-in system and created a simple one-page guide for coworkers.
- Strong sense of empathy and respect for patients from diverse backgrounds. Growing up in a bilingual household and seeing my parents struggle to navigate the healthcare system, I take extra care to make sure patients understand what they need to do next and feel heard, not rushed.

Overall, my combination of hands-on clinic experience, bilingual communication skills, comfort with EHRs and scheduling systems, and genuine interest in improving patient experience makes me well-prepared for a Medical Office Administrator role at a community health center like yours.
